name: zitadel-idp
description: Zitadel self-hosted OIDC integration field guide — 40 documented quirks with proto-aligned examples verified against raw GitHub Zitadel v4.15.0. Captures the full set of high-friction issues discovered while integrating Zitadel v4.x as the IdP for JRC Brasil ERP — FirstInstance env placement, volume permissions, v1/v2 API split, tenant→orgId mapping, JWT validation traps over self-signed HTTPS (NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS, JWKS over HTTPS), loginV2 instance flag, silent-renew redirect URI byte-match, JWT access token sem profile claims, idempotent bootstrap 'No changes' 400, TLS-terminating reverse proxy, secure-context PKCE on LAN, boot-time signinSilent recursion, StrictMode + closure cancelled flag, post_logout_redirect_uri invalid, branding da Login UI v1 / privateLabelingSetting / 405 em /assets/v1, custom_login_text e LANG-lg4DP, F5 com InMemoryWebStorage, 401 storm pós --reset-zitadel por tsx-watch zumbi, regressão multi-app YAML que sobrescreve env vars OIDC_REDIRECT_URIS dinâmicas, Zitadel v2.66.x panic 'No master key provided' (fix com flag CLI '--masterkey'); v0.3.0 — full v2.66 → v4 upgrade runbook + API v1 → v2 Connect protocol mapping; v0.4.0 — proto-aligned payloads verificados contra raw GitHub v4.15.0 (CreateApplication discriminator é oidcConfiguration top-level com applicationType / developmentMode internos, NÃO oidc com appType / devMode); per-service ListResponse field names; CreateAuthorization shape; AlreadyExisting matcher; quirk 28 — Login UI v2 auto-provisioning quebrado em v4.15.0 (zitadel/zitadel#8910 / #9293, mitigação canônica é Path B do quirk 25 com loginV2.required: false). v0.6.0 — Console UI human-user creation pitfalls (3 new quirks: 33 — Add Human User auto-truncates Username to email local-part; 34 — userLoginMustBeDomain=true stamps loginNames irreversibly; 35 — E-mail Verificado checkbox OFF by default with no SMTP). v0.7.0 — TWO production-cutover quirks from validade_bateria_estoque PR #9: quirk 36 — backend container that validates JWT needs `extra_hosts: idp.<domain>:host-gateway` when IdP is co-located on a VPS with unreliable hairpin NAT; quirk 37 — frontend defense-in-depth against 401-storm-revokes-session requires THREE coordinated layers (ApiClient dedupe + TanStack retry filter + apiclient:unauthorized listener with auth-route guard). NEW v0.8.0 — THREE smoke-e2e CI quirks from validade_bateria_estoque PR #10: quirk 38 — `ZITADEL_FIRSTINSTANCE_PATPATH` bind mount EACCES on GHA runner (uid 1000 vs uid 1001 + 0755) cascades into a misleading `unique_constraints_pkey` on retries (looks like quirk 28 but isn't — the partial setup leaves `instance_domain` in the eventstore); fix is `mkdir -p && chmod 0777` BEFORE `docker compose up`; quirk 39 — default password policy requires all 4 char classes (upper/lower/digit/symbol), `openssl rand -hex` is lowercase-only and dies with `COMMA-VoaRj` on AddHumanUser; fix is structured prefix `Aa1!` + alphanum random tail; quirk 40 — `zitadel-login` (Login UI v2 Next.js) needs ~90s+ for first healthcheck render on small CI runners, so `up --wait --wait-timeout 120` for the whole stack times out even though Zitadel itself is Healthy; fix is to scope `up --wait` to `zitadel-db zitadel-init zitadel` since bootstrap and REST tests don't exercise Login UI. Plus a new 'Smoke-e2e plumbing checklist for GHA' (chmod + scope --wait + 4-class password + Prisma generate/migrate deploy + capture project_id from bootstrap.json into $GITHUB_ENV + always-dump on-failure logs) covering the 4-5 layers of breakage that rot under `continue-on-error: true` until someone tries to bring the job back to green. Bundles a working docker-compose.zitadel.yml, idempotent bootstrap-zitadel.ts and reset-zitadel.sh. Use whenever starting Zitadel integration, debugging 4xx on Zitadel APIs, validating Zitadel JWTs in Node/TypeScript, customizing the hosted Login UI, wiring an SPA via OIDC PKCE, planning a v2.66→v4 upgrade, refactoring callers from API v1 to v2 against v4.15+, hitting the Login UI v2 deploy bug, onboarding TI staff to provision human users via Console, debugging time-correlated 401-storms in production self-hosted backends, hardening an SPA against RT-reuse session revocation, or destrava a smoke-e2e CI job stuck behind layers of bind-mount/policy/healthcheck breakage.
